result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `KvJournal.cs`, and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

- **`Get`** scans the file line by line, splitting each line at the *first* `=` (so values may contain `=`), and returns the last value seen for the key — or `""` if the file doesn't exist or the key never appears.
- **`CountKeys`** collects key names into a `HashSet` and returns its size, or `0` when the file is missing.
- **`Compact`** builds first-appearance key order plus a last-value map in one pass, then rewrites the file as one `key=value\n` line per distinct key. If the file doesn't exist it returns without creating it, and compacting an already-compact journal rewrites identical contents.

`Set` is untouched, all I/O goes directly through `System.IO.File` on each call (no caching), and the public surface matches the pinned signatures: `Set(string, string, string)`, `Get(string, string) → string`, `CountKeys(string) → int`, `Compact(string)`.

(Unrelated note: this session shows claude.ai Gmail/Calendar/Drive connectors as needing authorization via your claude.ai connector settings — nothing in this task required them.)
